sutta: monks not having yet visited it is not fit to visit a family of sevenfold conduct nor having visited to sit down what 7 pleasantly they rise not pleasantly they salute not nor pleasantly offer a seed they hide it from much they give little from choice food they give course food without respect they give not respectfully verily monks it is not fit to visit a family of this 7-fold conduct that's the end of the suta
commentary: in the 1 of the earliest uttars we read the buddha said that there are 4 types of persons that we should be careful in our conduct towards the first 1 is the buddha second 1 is disciples of the buddha that means monks and nuns third is mother the fourth is father these are the 4 fields of married or g married so in our conduct was these people we have to be very careful now it is better to be [Music] overly good than overly bad towards them and in the mangala sutta they stated that the sight of monks is a blessing so when a monk visits a lay person the family receive blessings and especially if the monk comes along on arms round in the battle and then they receive more blessings if they show respect to the monk they rise up they offer a seed they offer food etc and here in this utah the buddha said if this family does not do these things then a monk should not visit such a family when they see the manga they don't show respect man they don't rise up they don't offer a seed if they have food they hide it and then if they have much food they give very little if they have good food they give a poor food and then when they give they don't give respectfully so uh if if us there is such a family here it is better not for a monk to visit them because in when your mom visits such a family instead of getting married now that family i might create be married by uh by their conduct towards the monks the next utah is

without respect they give not respectfully
